page: Amazon Web Services g5g.2xlarge is an ARM64-based virtual machine powered by an AWS Graviton2 processor using the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz, with 64 KB L1D cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. For graphics and accelerated workloads, it includes a single NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. It does not feature local NVMe storage and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across several compute and memory metrics, alongside some weak areas. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 12132.81, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 5407.0, both falling in the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 472.81 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 121.66 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 5224.12. Cryptographic and compression tasks also show lower relative performance, with a Passmark encryption score of 1082.49 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 20.3 GB/sec.

The integrated NVIDIA T4G GPU provides hardware acceleration, though LLM inference speeds are limited. Text generation using a small 135M model reaches 36.51 tokens/sec, while a medium 7B model achieves 6.16 tokens/sec, both rating as poor. Ray tracing and image processing benchmarks score in the average tier at 7109.0 and 4112.0 respectively. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for ARM-based graphics workloads, Android game streaming, and entry-level GPU-accelerated applications. It is appropriate for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and lightweight GPU-accelerated tasks, but is less optimal for heavy LLM inference or high-performance single-threaded computing.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.2xlarge is an ARM64-based instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton2 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12132.81 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 5407.0, though single-core and cryptographic performance are relatively weak. The hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for ARM-native graphics applications, Android game streaming, and lightweight GPU-accelerated workloads. It is appropriate for static web serving, database operations, and entry-level machine learning tasks that do not require heavy LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.2xlarge is an ARM64 server with 8 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU. It is designed for cost-efficient ARM-native graphics and lightweight GPU workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.2xlarge features 8 ARM64 cores, 16 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for cost-efficient graphics and GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based virtual machine featuring dedicated Graviton processors and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for cost-effective graphics and accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
NVIDIA Turing T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM
16.0 GB of system memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average multi-core performance with 12132.81 stress-ng score
Appropriate for ARM-native graphics, web serving, and database operations
